enrichment.short_description: ServiceTitan is a cloud-based operations platform for trades businesses, giving HVAC, plumbing, electrical, roofing, and similar companies one system for scheduling, dispatch, CRM, marketing, invoicing, and payments.
enrichment.pricing_model: Custom quote-based pricing, typically billed per technician on an annual contract
enrichment.starting_price: From approximately $398 per month, custom quote required
enrichment.company_size: Approximately 2,900 employees, publicly traded on Nasdaq as TTAN
enrichment.seo_title: ServiceTitan Review 2026: Features, Pricing and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See what ServiceTitan costs, who it is built for, and how its scheduling, dispatch, CRM, and marketing tools compare for HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and other trades businesses.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: ServiceTitan is a cloud-based, all-in-one operations platform purpose-built for residential and commercial trades businesses, including HVAC, plumbing, electrical, roofing, garage door, and chimney companies. Founded in 2007 by Ara Mahdessian and Vahe Kuzoyan, the company is headquartered in Glendale, California, and became publicly traded on the Nasdaq under the ticker TTAN after its December 2024 IPO, which raised roughly 625 million dollars and valued the company at approximately 8.9 billion dollars. ServiceTitan replaces the paper-based and spreadsheet workflows many trades businesses relied on with a unified system covering call booking, dispatch and scheduling, technician mobile apps, CRM, marketing automation, pricebook and estimate management, invoicing, financing, inventory, and reporting. The platform is aimed primarily at mid-size to large trades businesses with multiple technicians and trucks rather than solo operators, and pricing is not published; it is quoted per business based on technician count and modules used, with third-party sources reporting typical starting costs around 398 dollars per month plus onboarding fees that can run from several thousand to tens of thousands of dollars. ServiceTitan integrates with QuickBooks, financing providers, and marketing channels, and is widely used across North America. Competitors include Housecall Pro, Jobber, and FieldEdge. As of 2024 ServiceTitan employed approximately 2,900 people. Note: ServiceTitan is unrelated to ServiceNow, a separate enterprise IT service management company.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is ServiceTitan
paragraphs: ServiceTitan is a cloud-based business management platform built specifically for the trades: HVAC, plumbing, electrical, roofing, garage door, chimney, and similar home and commercial service companies. Rather than stitching together separate scheduling, CRM, invoicing, and marketing tools, ServiceTitan puts all of these workflows into a single system that call center staff, dispatchers, technicians, and owners share in real time.

Founded in 2007 by Ara Mahdessian and Vahe Kuzoyan and headquartered in Glendale, California, ServiceTitan grew into one of the largest software vendors serving the trades industry before going public on the Nasdaq in December 2024 under the ticker TTAN. It is generally positioned for established trades businesses running multiple trucks and technicians rather than solo operators just getting started.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: ServiceTitan covers the full job lifecycle: call booking and intake, drag-and-drop dispatch and scheduling boards, a technician mobile app with route optimization, a configurable pricebook for flat-rate pricing and upsells, and on-site invoicing with integrated payment and financing options.

On the back office side, the platform includes CRM with full customer and equipment history, marketing automation tied to call tracking and campaign ROI reporting, inventory and truck stock management, and business intelligence dashboards that roll performance up across technicians, crews, and locations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: ServiceTitan does not publish fixed pricing tiers. Costs are quoted per business based on the number of technicians, the modules enabled, and contract length, and are typically billed under an annual agreement rather than month to month.

Third-party operator reports and review sites cite starting costs around 398 dollars per month, with all-in per-technician costs commonly landing between roughly 300 and 500-plus dollars per month, plus a separate onboarding and implementation fee that can range from a few thousand to tens of thousands of dollars depending on company size.

features.title: Scheduling and Dispatch
features.description: A visual dispatch board lets office staff assign and reassign jobs to technicians in real time based on skills, location, and availability.

features.title: Technician Mobile App
features.description: Field technicians access job details, customer history, pricebook items, and payment collection from a dedicated mobile app.

features.title: CRM and Customer History
features.description: Every customer record includes past jobs, equipment installed, service agreements, and communication history.

features.title: Marketing Automation and Call Tracking
features.description: Tracks marketing spend against booked revenue and automates follow-up campaigns and review requests.

features.title: Pricebook and Flat-Rate Pricing
features.description: A configurable pricebook standardizes flat-rate pricing and upsell options across technicians and crews.

features.title: Invoicing, Payments, and Financing
features.description: Technicians can invoice, collect payment, and offer customer financing options directly from the field.

features.title: Inventory and Fleet Management
features.description: Tracks truck stock, warehouse inventory, and purchase orders tied to specific jobs.

features.title: Reporting and Business Analytics
features.description: Dashboards roll up performance across technicians, crews, and locations for owners and managers.

pros: Extremely deep, trades-specific feature set spanning nearly every back-office and field workflow
Strong marketing and call-tracking tools tied directly to revenue attribution
Well-built mobile app designed around how field technicians actually work
Established, publicly traded vendor with a large customer base across the trades industry
cons: Pricing is not published and can be expensive once onboarding fees are included
Steep learning curve and lengthy implementation timeline for new customers
Long-term annual contracts can make switching providers difficult
Built primarily for larger multi-technician operations, more than many small or solo businesses need

faqs.question: What is ServiceTitan used for?
faqs.answer: ServiceTitan is used by trades businesses like HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and roofing companies to manage scheduling, dispatch, CRM, marketing, invoicing, and payments in one platform.

faqs.question: How much does ServiceTitan cost?
faqs.answer: ServiceTitan uses custom, quote-based pricing rather than published plans. Third-party sources report starting costs around 398 dollars per month, with all-in per-technician costs often between roughly 300 and 500-plus dollars per month, plus onboarding fees.

faqs.question: Does ServiceTitan offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: ServiceTitan does not publicly advertise a self-serve free trial; prospective customers typically go through a sales demo and custom quote process.

faqs.question: What industries does ServiceTitan serve?
faqs.answer: ServiceTitan primarily serves residential and commercial trades businesses including HVAC, plumbing, electrical, roofing, garage door, and chimney companies.

faqs.question: Is ServiceTitan the same as ServiceNow?
faqs.answer: No. ServiceTitan is trades-focused field service management software, while ServiceNow is an unrelated enterprise IT service management company. The names are similar but the companies and products are completely different.

faqs.question: Does ServiceTitan integrate with QuickBooks?
faqs.answer: Yes, ServiceTitan offers integration with QuickBooks for syncing accounting and financial data.

faqs.question: Is ServiceTitan publicly traded?
faqs.answer: Yes. ServiceTitan went public on the Nasdaq under the ticker TTAN in December 2024.

faqs.question: What size business is ServiceTitan best suited for?
faqs.answer: ServiceTitan is generally best suited for established trades businesses with multiple technicians and trucks rather than solo operators or very small teams.

use_cases.title: HVAC Company Dispatch and Scheduling
use_cases.description: An HVAC company with multiple crews uses ServiceTitan's dispatch board to assign emergency and scheduled jobs to the nearest available technician.

use_cases.title: Plumbing Business Marketing ROI
use_cases.description: A plumbing company tracks which marketing campaigns and phone numbers generate booked revenue using ServiceTitan's call tracking and reporting.

use_cases.title: Electrical Contractor Field Payments
use_cases.description: An electrical contractor's technicians invoice and collect payment on-site immediately after completing a job using the mobile app.

enrichment.short_description: Sessionize is a call for papers and speaker management platform that helps conference and event organizers collect session submissions, review and vote on proposals, build event schedules, and manage speaker profiles.
enrichment.pricing_model: Free for non-commercial community events; flat per-event fee for commercial events; custom bulk pricing for agencies
enrichment.starting_price: Free for community events; $499 USD per event for commercial events
enrichment.seo_title: Sessionize Review 2026: CFP and Speaker Management Pricing and Features
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Learn how Sessionize handles call for papers, speaker management, and event scheduling, plus its free community-event pricing and $499 commercial-event fee.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Sessionize is a software-as-a-service platform focused on call for papers (CFP) management, speaker management, and event scheduling for tech conferences, meetups, and community events. Founded in 2017 and headquartered in Zagreb, Croatia, Sessionize is run by a small, privately held team of roughly 2 to 10 people, yet the platform is used by thousands of events across more than 120 countries and is a de facto standard among developer and technology conferences worldwide. Organizers use Sessionize to create custom CFP submission forms, collect and review speaker proposals, run voting, communicate with speakers, and publish a public event schedule and speaker directory. Speakers get a persistent profile they can reuse to submit talks to many different events without re-entering their bio, photo, and session abstracts each time. Sessionize is free for non-commercial, no participation-fee community events, provided the organizer allows Sessionize backlinking; commercial and for-profit events pay a flat fee of 499 US dollars per event occurrence, and organizations running five or more events per year can arrange custom bulk pricing with pre-purchased activation codes. There is no credit card required to try Sessionize, and a fully functional test mode lets organizers evaluate the platform before paying. Competitors in the event and CFP management space include Sessionboard and Papercall.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Sessionize
paragraphs: Sessionize is a specialized platform for running a call for papers (CFP) process and managing the speakers and schedule for a conference, meetup, or other community event. Instead of collecting talk submissions through email or spreadsheets, organizers use Sessionize to publish a submission form, review proposals, and assemble a final schedule.

Founded in 2017 and based in Zagreb, Croatia, Sessionize is run by a very small, privately held team, but it has become widely used across the technology conference circuit, supporting thousands of events in over 120 countries. Its strength comes from being purpose-built for the CFP and speaker workflow rather than being a general event management or ticketing platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Organizers can build custom submission forms with their own fields, review and score incoming session proposals, run voting among reviewers, and message accepted or declined speakers directly through the platform. A public event page automatically lists the schedule and speaker bios once sessions are confirmed.

Speakers benefit from a reusable Sessionize profile that stores their photo, bio, and past sessions, so submitting to a new event does not require re-entering the same information. This shared speaker directory is also one of the reasons Sessionize has become popular across many overlapping tech communities.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Sessionize is free for community events that do not charge a participation fee, as long as the organizer allows Sessionize backlinking on the event page; it is not intended for internal, promotional, or profit-generating events.

Commercial and for-profit events pay a flat 499 US dollar fee per event occurrence, which unlocks the full feature set and priority email support. Organizations running five or more events per year can negotiate custom bulk pricing using pre-purchased activation codes, and no credit card is required to explore the platform in test mode first.

features.title: Custom CFP Submission Forms
features.description: Organizers build tailored submission forms to collect talk proposals with their own required fields.

features.title: Speaker Profile Directory
features.description: Speakers maintain one reusable profile with bio, photo, and past sessions across multiple events.

features.title: Session Review and Voting
features.description: Reviewers can score, comment on, and vote on submitted session proposals.

features.title: Event Schedule Builder
features.description: Accepted sessions can be arranged into a public-facing event schedule automatically.

features.title: Speaker Communication Tools
features.description: Built-in messaging lets organizers notify speakers of acceptance, rejection, or schedule details.

features.title: Public Event and Speaker Pages
features.description: Each event gets a public page listing sessions, speakers, and schedule details.

features.title: Multi-Event Speaker Reuse
features.description: Speakers can apply to many different Sessionize-powered events without re-entering their information.

features.title: Submission Analytics
features.description: Organizers can review submission volume and reviewer activity throughout the CFP window.

pros: Purpose-built specifically for CFP and speaker management rather than general event ticketing
Free for non-commercial community events
Widely adopted across the tech conference circuit, so many speakers already have profiles
No credit card required to test the platform before paying
cons: Commercial event fee is a flat 499 dollars per event regardless of event size
Feature set is narrowly focused on CFP and scheduling, not full event ticketing or registration
Very small company size means limited support bandwidth compared to larger event platforms
Bulk and agency pricing is not published and requires a custom conversation

faqs.question: What is Sessionize used for?
faqs.answer: Sessionize is used to manage a conference or event's call for papers, review and vote on speaker submissions, and build the public event schedule and speaker directory.

faqs.question: Is Sessionize free?
faqs.answer: Sessionize is free for non-commercial community events with no participation fee. Commercial or for-profit events pay a flat 499 US dollar fee per event.

faqs.question: How much does Sessionize cost for a paid conference?
faqs.answer: Commercial events pay 499 US dollars plus tax per event occurrence, which unlocks the full Sessionize feature set.

faqs.question: Do speakers need to pay to use Sessionize?
faqs.answer: No. Speakers create a free profile and submit talks to events at no cost; only event organizers running commercial events pay a fee.

faqs.question: Where is Sessionize based?
faqs.answer: Sessionize was founded in 2017 and is headquartered in Zagreb, Croatia.

faqs.question: Does Sessionize require a credit card to try?
faqs.answer: No. Sessionize offers a fully functional test mode that does not require a credit card.

faqs.question: Can agencies running multiple events get discounted pricing?
faqs.answer: Yes. Organizations running five or more events per year can arrange custom bulk pricing with pre-purchased activation codes.

faqs.question: Is Sessionize only for tech conferences?
faqs.answer: Sessionize is most widely adopted in the technology and developer conference community, but it can be used for any event that needs CFP and speaker management.

use_cases.title: Developer Conference CFP
use_cases.description: A mid-size developer conference uses Sessionize to open a call for papers, have reviewers score submissions, and publish the final agenda.

use_cases.title: Community Meetup Speaker Directory
use_cases.description: A recurring local meetup group uses Sessionize's free community-event tier to collect talk proposals each month.

use_cases.title: Multi-Event Agency Management
use_cases.description: An events agency running a dozen commercial conferences per year uses Sessionize's bulk activation codes to manage CFPs across all of them.
use_cases.audience: Event agencies and corporate event teams

features: For non-commercial events with no participation fee
Full CFP and speaker management feature set
Requires a Sessionize backlink on the event page
Not for internal, promotional, or profit-generating events
No credit card required to start in test mode
For agencies and corporations running five or more events per year
Pre-purchased activation codes used as needed

enrichment.short_description: Setmore is a free online appointment scheduling and booking platform that lets service businesses accept online bookings, manage staff calendars, send reminders, take payments, and host video appointments.
enrichment.pricing_model: Freemium, with a free plan for up to 4 users and paid per-user monthly plans
enrichment.starting_price: Free for up to 4 users; Pro plan from $5 per user per month billed annually
enrichment.company_size: Small, privately held team, reported at roughly 20 to 30 employees
enrichment.seo_title: Setmore Review 2026: Free Appointment Scheduling Pricing and Features
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See how Setmore's free and Pro appointment scheduling plans work, what features are included, and how it compares for booking, reminders, and payments.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Setmore is a cloud-based appointment scheduling and online booking platform designed for small and medium service businesses such as salons, spas, medical and wellness practices, consultants, and home service providers. Founded in 2011, Setmore is run by a small, privately held team reported at roughly 20 to 30 employees. The platform is best known for its genuinely free plan, which supports up to 4 staff users, a branded online booking page, up to 200 monthly appointments, email confirmations and reminders, iOS and Android apps, and integrations with tools like Mailchimp, HubSpot, Zapier, and Google Analytics 4. Businesses that outgrow the free tier can upgrade to the Pro plan, priced from 5 US dollars per user per month on annual billing, or 12 dollars per user per month billed monthly, which adds unlimited appointments, SMS reminders, two-way calendar sync, recurring appointments, video appointments, custom availability rules, and API access. Setmore also offers an optional Live Receptionist add-on for 99 US dollars per month for US customers who want human call answering. Payment processing is supported through Square, Stripe, PayPal, and LawPay. Setmore competes with tools like Calendly, Acuity Scheduling, and Square Appointments, and is frequently recommended for businesses that want a no-cost entry point into online booking before committing to a paid scheduling tool.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Setmore
paragraphs: Setmore is an online appointment scheduling platform aimed at small and medium service businesses, including salons, spas, healthcare and wellness practices, consultants, tutors, and home service providers. It provides a branded online booking page that customers can use to book appointments directly, without phone calls or back-and-forth emails.

Founded in 2011, Setmore is a small, privately held company best known in the scheduling software category for offering a genuinely usable free plan rather than a stripped-down trial, which has made it a common recommendation for businesses booking their first online scheduling tool.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Core features include staff and service management, a customizable booking page, automated email and SMS reminders, two-way calendar sync with Google and Outlook, recurring appointments, and built-in video appointments for remote consultations.

Setmore also supports payment collection through Square, Stripe, PayPal, and LawPay, mobile apps for iOS and Android, and integrations with Mailchimp, HubSpot, Zapier, and Google Analytics 4, plus an optional US-based live receptionist add-on for businesses that want human call handling alongside online booking.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Setmore's Free plan costs nothing and supports up to 4 staff users, up to 200 monthly appointments, a branded booking page, reminders, and mobile apps, with no time limit on how long a business can stay on it.

The Pro plan starts at 5 US dollars per user per month when billed annually, or 12 dollars per user per month billed monthly, and removes the appointment cap and Setmore branding while adding SMS reminders, two-way calendar sync, recurring and video appointments, and API access. A Live Receptionist add-on is available for 99 US dollars per month for US-based businesses.

Low-cost Pro plan starting at 5 dollars per user per month annually
Built-in video appointments and payment processing without extra software
Broad integration support including Zapier, Mailchimp, and HubSpot
cons: Free plan is capped at 200 appointments per month and 4 users
Monthly (non-annual) Pro pricing is notably higher at 12 dollars per user
Headquarters and company ownership details are not consistently published
Advanced customization and reporting are more limited than larger scheduling platforms

faqs.question: Is Setmore really free?
faqs.answer: Yes. Setmore's Free plan supports up to 4 users and 200 monthly appointments at no cost, with no trial time limit.

faqs.question: How much does Setmore Pro cost?
faqs.answer: Setmore Pro starts at 5 US dollars per user per month when billed annually, or 12 dollars per user per month when billed monthly.

faqs.question: Does Setmore support online payments?
faqs.answer: Yes, Setmore integrates with Square, Stripe, PayPal, and LawPay for accepting online and in-person payments.

faqs.question: Can Setmore send text message reminders?
faqs.answer: SMS reminders are available on the Pro plan; the Free plan includes email confirmations and reminders.

faqs.question: Does Setmore offer video appointments?
faqs.answer: Yes, video appointments are included as part of the Pro plan.

faqs.question: When was Setmore founded?
faqs.answer: Setmore was founded in 2011.

faqs.question: Does Setmore have mobile apps?
faqs.answer: Yes, Setmore offers native apps for both iOS and Android.

faqs.question: What is the Setmore Live Receptionist add-on?
faqs.answer: It is an optional add-on, available for US customers at 99 US dollars per month, that provides human receptionists to answer calls and book appointments.

enrichment.short_description: SevenRooms is a hospitality operations and guest experience platform that helps restaurants, hotels, and nightlife venues manage reservations, waitlists, table management, guest CRM, and marketing in one system.
enrichment.pricing_model: Custom quote-based pricing per venue, billed on an annual contract
enrichment.starting_price: Approximately $499 per month per venue, custom quote required
enrichment.headquarters: New York City, New York, United States
enrichment.seo_title: SevenRooms Review 2026: Reservation and Guest CRM Pricing and Features
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See how SevenRooms handles restaurant and hospitality reservations, waitlists, guest CRM, and marketing, plus its pricing and 2025 DoorDash acquisition.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SevenRooms is a cloud-based reservation, waitlist, table management, and guest experience platform built for restaurants, hotels, and nightlife and entertainment venues. It was founded in 2011 by Joel Montaniel, Allison Page, and Kinesh Patel, and is headquartered in New York City, with additional offices in the UK, Dubai, Australia, Singapore, and Hong Kong; the company employs roughly 350 to 400 people globally. SevenRooms lets hospitality operators manage bookings and waitlists, assign and optimize tables, capture detailed guest profiles and preferences, run email and text marketing campaigns, and access a reservation marketplace with tens of millions of DoorDash and Deliveroo diners at no per-cover fee. Pricing is not published; it is quoted per venue, and third-party operator reports put the core platform starting around 499 US dollars per month on an annual contract, with add-ons such as WhatsApp messaging priced separately. SevenRooms has raised roughly 74.4 million dollars in funding across five rounds, including an 8 million dollar round led by Comcast Ventures in 2017 and a 50 million dollar Series B led by Providence Strategic Growth in 2020. In May 2025, DoorDash announced an agreement to acquire SevenRooms for approximately 1.2 billion dollars in cash, closely integrating restaurant reservation and guest data with DoorDash's delivery and marketplace business. Competitors include OpenTable, Resy, and Tock.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is SevenRooms
paragraphs: SevenRooms is a hospitality operations platform that gives restaurants, hotels, and nightlife venues one system for managing reservations, waitlists, table assignments, guest profiles, and marketing. It is built for operators who want to understand and market to individual guests, not just fill tables.

Founded in 2011 by Joel Montaniel, Allison Page, and Kinesh Patel, SevenRooms is headquartered in New York City and operates internationally with offices in the UK, Dubai, Australia, Singapore, and Hong Kong. In May 2025, DoorDash announced an agreement to acquire SevenRooms for approximately 1.2 billion dollars, tying the platform's guest and reservation data more closely to DoorDash's delivery and marketplace business.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: SevenRooms provides branded online reservations and waitlist management, drag-and-drop table management, and a guest CRM that stores dining history, preferences, and special occasion notes so staff can personalize service on repeat visits.

Marketing tools include automated email and text campaigns, reputation management for reviews, and access to a reservation marketplace with tens of millions of DoorDash and Deliveroo users at no cover fee. Optional add-ons extend the platform with WhatsApp and text marketing, voice AI, event management, and API integrations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SevenRooms does not publish fixed pricing; venues request a custom quote, and pricing is set per venue based on the modules and volume required, typically under an annual contract.

Third-party operator reports and review sites indicate the core platform commonly starts around 499 US dollars per month per venue, with optional add-ons such as WhatsApp messaging priced separately at roughly 100 dollars per month.

enrichment.short_description: Shaarli is a free, open-source, self-hosted bookmarking and link-sharing tool written in PHP that stores links in flat files instead of a database, making it lightweight to install on personal servers.
enrichment.pricing_model: Free and open source; self-hosted with no paid tiers
enrichment.seo_title: Shaarli Review 2026: Free Self-Hosted Bookmarking Tool Features
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Learn what Shaarli is, how the free open-source, database-free bookmarking tool works, and why self-hosters use it instead of hosted bookmark services.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Shaarli is a free, open-source, self-hosted bookmarking and link-sharing service written in PHP. It was originally created by French developer Sebastien Sauvage and first released in 2011 as a lightweight, database-free alternative to hosted bookmarking services like the discontinued Delicious. Because Shaarli stores all bookmarks in flat files rather than a database, it can be installed on almost any shared or personal web host with minimal setup and no database server required. The community-maintained fork, hosted at github.com slash shaarli slash Shaarli, is now the actively developed version of the project, with roughly 3,900 GitHub stars, more than 400 forks, and over 50 tagged releases, the latest being version 0.16.3. Shaarli is designed for a single user rather than teams, and is commonly used as a personal knowledge base, a public or private link blog, an RSS-readable bookmark archive, or a lightweight alternative to browser bookmark syncing. Key features include one-click bookmarklets for saving links, tagging, full-text search, RSS and ATOM feed generation, a plugin system, LDAP login support for single-user authentication, and a responsive interface that degrades gracefully without JavaScript. The interface has been translated into multiple languages including English, French, German, and Japanese. As free software with no company behind it, Shaarli has no pricing, no company size, and no headquarters; it is maintained by volunteer open-source contributors.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Shaarli
paragraphs: Shaarli is a free, open-source bookmarking and link-sharing tool that users install on their own web server rather than signing up for a hosted account. It was created by French developer Sebastien Sauvage and first released in 2011 as a minimalist, database-free alternative to hosted bookmarking services.

The actively maintained version today is a community fork hosted on GitHub, which has continued development after the original project slowed down. Because Shaarli stores links in flat files instead of a database, it can run on almost any basic PHP web host, which is part of why it remains popular among self-hosting enthusiasts.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Shaarli offers a bookmarklet for saving links with one click, tagging and full-text search across saved links, RSS and ATOM feed generation, and a plugin system for extending functionality. It supports LDAP login for single-user authentication and a responsive interface that still works with JavaScript disabled.

Because it is self-hosted, users retain full control of their bookmark data rather than depending on a third-party service that could shut down or change its terms. The interface is available in multiple languages, including English, French, German, and Japanese.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Shaarli is completely free and open source, distributed under a free software license with the source code publicly available on GitHub. There are no paid tiers, subscriptions, or premium feature unlocks.

The only ongoing cost is whatever web hosting the user chooses to run Shaarli on, since it must be self-hosted; some third-party hosting providers also offer managed Shaarli hosting for a fee they set independently of the Shaarli project itself.

features.title: Flat-File Storage
features.description: Bookmarks are stored in flat files rather than a database, simplifying installation and hosting.

features.title: One-Click Bookmarklet
features.description: A browser bookmarklet lets users save the current page as a bookmark in one click.

features.title: Tagging and Full-Text Search
features.description: Bookmarks can be tagged and searched across titles, descriptions, and content.

features.title: RSS and ATOM Feeds
features.description: Automatically generates feeds so others can follow a user's shared links.

features.title: Plugin System
features.description: A plugin architecture allows the community to extend Shaarli's core functionality.

features.title: Self-Hosted Privacy and Control
features.description: Users fully control their own bookmark data on their own server.

features.title: LDAP Single-User Login
features.description: Supports LDAP authentication for the single-user login model.

features.title: Multilingual Interface
features.description: The interface is available in English, French, German, and Japanese, among other translations.

pros: Completely free and open source with no paid tiers
No database server required, making it lightweight to install
Full data ownership through self-hosting
Active open-source community maintaining the project with regular releases
cons: Requires technical skill to install, configure, and maintain a web server
Designed for a single user rather than team or multi-user use
No official paid support channel
Interface is intentionally minimalist rather than feature-rich compared to hosted bookmarking apps

faqs.question: What is Shaarli?
faqs.answer: Shaarli is a free, open-source, self-hosted bookmarking and link-sharing tool written in PHP that stores data in flat files instead of a database.

faqs.question: Is Shaarli free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Shaarli is free and open-source software with no paid tiers; the only cost is the web hosting you choose to run it on.

faqs.question: Do I need a database to run Shaarli?
faqs.answer: No, Shaarli stores bookmarks in flat files and does not require a separate database server.

faqs.question: Can multiple people share one Shaarli instance?
faqs.answer: Shaarli is designed as a single-user tool, though multiple people can view public links; it is not built for multi-user team collaboration.

faqs.question: How do I install Shaarli?
faqs.answer: Shaarli is installed by downloading it from GitHub and deploying it on a PHP-capable web host, following the setup wizard on first run.

faqs.question: Is Shaarli still actively maintained?
faqs.answer: Yes, the community fork on GitHub is actively maintained, with the latest release being version 0.16.3 as of 2026.

faqs.question: What is the difference between the original Shaarli and the community fork?
faqs.answer: The original Shaarli was created by developer Sebastien Sauvage in 2011; a community fork on GitHub has since become the actively developed version of the project.

faqs.question: Does Shaarli support tags and search?
faqs.answer: Yes, Shaarli supports tagging bookmarks and full-text search across saved links.

use_cases.title: Personal Bookmark Archive
use_cases.description: A developer self-hosts Shaarli to save and tag technical articles for later reference.

use_cases.title: Public Link Blog
use_cases.description: A blogger or journalist uses Shaarli as a public link blog to share and comment on interesting articles.

use_cases.title: Lightweight Research Reference Tool
use_cases.description: A student or researcher uses Shaarli to organize and search sources for a project without relying on a third-party service.

features: Full source code and all features included
Some third-party providers offer managed Shaarli hosting
Pricing set independently by hosting providers, not by the Shaarli project

enrichment.short_description: Shaper is the company behind Shaper Origin, a handheld CNC router, and its companion software Shaper Studio and Shaper Hub, which let makers design, plan, and cut precise woodworking and fabrication projects.
enrichment.pricing_model: One-time hardware purchase for the Shaper Origin router, with free companion design software
enrichment.starting_price: Shaper Origin starts at $2,899; Shaper Studio and Shaper Hub software are free to use
enrichment.seo_title: Shaper Review 2026: Origin CNC Router and Shaper Hub Software
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See how Shaper Origin's handheld CNC router works with the free Shaper Studio and Shaper Hub software, plus current pricing and key features.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Shaper is the maker of Shaper Origin, a handheld CNC router that pairs the portability of a standard woodworking router with the positional accuracy of a full CNC machine, along with two companion software products, Shaper Studio and Shaper Hub. The company is commonly cited as founded around 2011 or 2012 by MIT-trained engineers Alec Rivers and Ilan Moyer, and is headquartered in San Francisco, California, with a further office in Stuttgart, Germany, reflecting its manufacturing partnership with German tool maker Festool, which assembles the Origin hardware. Rather than requiring a fixed CNC bed and G-code programming, Origin uses an onboard camera, a printed reference tape system called ShaperTape, and a touchscreen display so a user can trace or auto-correct a cut path freehand while the tool keeps the bit within a small tolerance of the intended design. Shaper Studio is the company's browser and device-based design tool for creating and preparing cut files, while Shaper Hub, at hub.shapertools.com, is an online library where Origin owners can browse, download, and share community-made project designs. Both software products are provided free alongside hardware ownership rather than sold as a standalone subscription. The Shaper Origin hardware itself starts at 2,899 US dollars, with bundles that include a Workstation, Plate, or BenchPilot accessory system ranging up to roughly 5,306 dollars. Shaper primarily serves woodworkers, makers, and small fabrication shops rather than enterprise buyers.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Shaper
paragraphs: Shaper is a hardware and software company best known for Shaper Origin, a handheld CNC router that lets users trace or freehand a cutting path while the tool automatically corrects small deviations to keep the cut precise. Unlike a traditional CNC machine, Origin does not require a fixed bed or workpiece to be clamped inside a machine frame.

The company is commonly cited as founded around 2011 or 2012 by MIT-trained engineers Alec Rivers and Ilan Moyer, and is headquartered in San Francisco, California, with an additional office in Stuttgart, Germany tied to its manufacturing partnership with German tool maker Festool, which assembles the Origin hardware.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Origin uses an onboard camera and a printed reference tape system called ShaperTape to track the tool's position on the workpiece in real time, along with a touchscreen display showing the design overlaid on the material as the user cuts.

The companion software includes Shaper Studio, a browser and device-based design tool for creating and preparing cut files, and Shaper Hub, a free online library and community where Origin owners can browse, download, and share project designs made by other makers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: The base Shaper Origin router starts at 2,899 US dollars, with free shipping, excluding tax. Bundle configurations that add accessories like the Workstation, Plate, or BenchPilot systems range from roughly 3,199 to 5,306 dollars depending on the combination chosen.

Shaper Studio and Shaper Hub are provided free to Origin owners rather than sold as a separate subscription, so the primary cost of adopting the Shaper ecosystem is the one-time hardware purchase rather than a recurring software fee.

features.title: Handheld Auto-Correcting CNC Cutting
features.description: Origin retracts or nudges the cutting bit automatically to keep cuts within a small tolerance of the design.

features.title: Shaper Studio Design Software
features.description: A browser and device-based tool for creating and preparing project files for cutting.

features.title: Shaper Hub Project Library
features.description: A free online hub where Origin owners can browse, download, and share community project designs.

features.title: ShaperTape Positioning System
features.description: A printed reference tape and onboard camera track the tool's exact position on the material.

features.title: Touchscreen Onboard Display
features.description: Shows the design overlaid on the workpiece directly on the tool during cutting.

features.title: WiFi and USB File Transfer
features.description: Supports software updates and design file transfers over WiFi or USB.

features.title: Portable, No Fixed Bed Required
features.description: At roughly 21 pounds, Origin can be used directly on a workpiece without a dedicated CNC bed.

features.title: Festool Co-Engineered Motor
features.description: The router's motor and spindle are co-produced with German tool maker Festool.

pros: No traditional CNC programming or G-code knowledge required to get started
Portable design works directly on a workpiece without a fixed CNC bed
Shaper Studio and Shaper Hub software are included free with hardware ownership
Auto-correction technology reduces the risk of costly cutting mistakes
cons: High upfront hardware cost compared to basic routers or jigs
Software and project sharing are built around owning the physical Origin router
Some learning curve involved in mastering the tracing and cutting technique
Primarily useful for woodworking and fabrication rather than general office or business software needs

faqs.question: What is Shaper Origin?
faqs.answer: Shaper Origin is a handheld CNC router that combines the form factor of a traditional router with automatic path correction for CNC-level cutting precision.

faqs.question: Is Shaper Hub free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Shaper Hub is a free online library where Shaper Origin owners can browse, download, and share community project designs.

faqs.question: Do I need CNC experience to use Shaper Origin?
faqs.answer: No, Origin is designed so users can trace a design freehand while the tool automatically corrects small deviations, without needing to write CNC code.

faqs.question: What is ShaperTape?
faqs.answer: ShaperTape is a printed reference tape applied to the workpiece that, along with an onboard camera, helps Origin track its exact position while cutting.

faqs.question: How much does Shaper Origin cost?
faqs.answer: The base Shaper Origin starts at 2,899 US dollars, with bundles including accessories ranging up to roughly 5,306 dollars.

faqs.question: Who manufactures Shaper Origin?
faqs.answer: Shaper designs and engineers Origin in California, and the hardware is assembled by German tool maker Festool.

faqs.question: Can I share my designs with other Shaper owners?
faqs.answer: Yes, Shaper Hub lets Origin owners upload and share their own project designs with the broader community.

faqs.question: Is Shaper Studio available without owning Origin?
faqs.answer: Shaper Studio is provided as companion software for Shaper Origin owners; check Shaper's official site for the most current details on standalone access.

enrichment.short_description: ShareFile is a secure, cloud-based file sharing and document workflow platform for businesses, especially accounting and legal firms, offering encrypted storage, client portals, e-signatures, and workflow automation.
enrichment.pricing_model: Tiered per-user subscription, billed monthly or annually, with a 3-user minimum
enrichment.starting_price: From $16.50 per user per month billed annually (Advanced plan)
enrichment.headquarters: Burlington, Massachusetts, United States (Progress Software); ShareFile originated in Raleigh, North Carolina
enrichment.seo_title: ShareFile Review 2026: Secure File Sharing Pricing and Features
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See how ShareFile's Advanced, Premium, Industry Advantage, and Virtual Data Room plans compare on price and features for secure business file sharing.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: ShareFile is a secure, cloud-based file sharing and document workflow platform built for professional services firms, particularly accounting, legal, financial services, and healthcare businesses that need to exchange sensitive documents with clients. It was created in November 2005 by self-taught programmer Jesse Lipson in Raleigh, North Carolina, after his web design clients asked for a secure alternative to FTP for exchanging files. ShareFile was acquired by Citrix Systems in 2011 and operated for over a decade as Citrix ShareFile before Progress Software Corporation, a Nasdaq-listed infrastructure software company headquartered in Burlington, Massachusetts, completed an 875 million dollar acquisition of the ShareFile business in October 2024, adding more than 86,000 customers to Progress's portfolio. The platform provides encrypted cloud storage, branded and password-protected client portals, real-time co-editing, granular access controls, e-signature collection, form building, task and workflow automation, and compliance support for standards such as HIPAA, FINRA, and SEC recordkeeping rules. ShareFile is sold on a per-user subscription with a 3-user minimum, or 5 users for the Virtual Data Room tier, across four public tiers, Advanced, Premium, Industry Advantage for accounting firms, and Virtual Data Room, ranging from roughly 16.50 to 77 dollars per user per month on annual billing. The product interface and apps support numerous languages including English, Spanish, French, German, Japanese, Dutch, Portuguese, Korean, Simplified Chinese, and Russian. Competitors include Box, Dropbox Business, and Egnyte.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is ShareFile
paragraphs: ShareFile is a secure, cloud-based file sharing and document workflow platform aimed at professional services businesses, particularly accounting, legal, financial services, and healthcare firms that regularly exchange sensitive documents with clients.

ShareFile was created in November 2005 by Jesse Lipson in Raleigh, North Carolina, acquired by Citrix Systems in 2011, and later acquired by Progress Software Corporation in October 2024 for 875 million dollars, bringing more than 86,000 customers into the Progress product portfolio.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: ShareFile provides encrypted cloud storage, branded and password-protected client portals, real-time co-editing, and granular access controls for managing who can view, edit, or download specific files and folders.

Higher tiers add e-signature collection, form building, task and workflow automation, and compliance support for standards such as HIPAA, FINRA, and SEC recordkeeping, plus accounting-specific workflow templates and a dedicated Virtual Data Room tier for confidential deal and due-diligence documents.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: ShareFile is sold as a per-user subscription with a 3-user minimum, across four published tiers, Advanced, Premium, Industry Advantage for accounting firms, and Virtual Data Room, which requires a 5-user minimum.

Annual billing lowers the per-user rate compared to monthly billing: Advanced starts at 16.50 dollars per user per month annually versus 18.15 monthly, Premium at 26.00 versus 28.60, Industry Advantage at 41.67 versus 45.83, and Virtual Data Room at 69.30 versus 77.00 per user per month.

features.title: Encrypted Cloud Storage
features.description: Files are stored with encryption and unlimited storage available on most plans.

features.title: Branded Client Portals
features.description: Password-protected, branded portals let clients securely upload and download files.

features.title: E-Signature Collection
features.description: Built-in e-signature tools let businesses collect signed documents without a separate tool.

features.title: Workflow Automation
features.description: Automates recurring document workflows such as engagement letters and client onboarding.

features.title: Real-Time Co-Editing
features.description: Multiple users can collaborate on documents within ShareFile in real time.

features.title: Compliance Support
features.description: Supports HIPAA, FINRA, and SEC recordkeeping requirements for regulated industries.

features.title: Virtual Data Rooms
features.description: A dedicated tier adds watermarking, granular permissions, and NDA enforcement for confidential deals.

features.title: Mobile and Desktop Sync Apps
features.description: Apps for major platforms keep files synced across devices.

pros: Strong compliance support for regulated industries like accounting, legal, and healthcare
Purpose-built Industry Advantage tier for accounting firm workflows
Wide language support across ten or more locales
Backed by Progress Software, a large, established, publicly traded vendor
cons: Public pricing requires a minimum of 3 users, which may not suit solo practitioners
Higher tiers such as Industry Advantage and Virtual Data Room can be expensive per user
Recent change of ownership to Progress Software may bring product roadmap changes
Feature set is heavily geared toward accounting and legal use cases, which may be more than general businesses need

faqs.question: What is ShareFile used for?
faqs.answer: ShareFile is used by businesses, especially accounting and legal firms, to securely store, share, and manage documents with clients through encrypted storage and branded client portals.

faqs.question: Who owns ShareFile?
faqs.answer: ShareFile was created in 2005, acquired by Citrix Systems in 2011, and acquired by Progress Software Corporation in October 2024 for 875 million dollars.

faqs.question: How much does ShareFile cost?
faqs.answer: ShareFile's Advanced plan starts at 16.50 US dollars per user per month billed annually, with higher tiers, Premium, Industry Advantage, and Virtual Data Room, priced up to roughly 77 dollars per user per month.

faqs.question: Does ShareFile support e-signatures?
faqs.answer: Yes, e-signature collection is included starting with the Premium plan.

faqs.question: Is ShareFile good for accounting firms?
faqs.answer: Yes, ShareFile offers an Industry Advantage tier built specifically for accounting firm workflows, including tax templates and bulk client onboarding.

faqs.question: What is the minimum number of users for ShareFile?
faqs.answer: Most ShareFile plans require a 3-user minimum, while the Virtual Data Room tier requires a 5-user minimum.

faqs.question: Does ShareFile support compliance requirements like HIPAA?
faqs.answer: Yes, ShareFile's Premium and higher tiers include support for HIPAA, FINRA, and SEC compliance requirements.

faqs.question: What languages does ShareFile support?
faqs.answer: ShareFile supports English, Spanish, French, German, Japanese, Dutch, Portuguese, Korean, Simplified Chinese, and Russian, among others.

enrichment.short_description: SharpCloud is a data visualization and strategic roadmapping platform that turns spreadsheets and project data into interactive visual stories for portfolio management, roadmapping, and scenario planning.
enrichment.pricing_model: Tiered, role-based annual licensing (Creator, Collaborator, Read-Only), plus a free version
enrichment.starting_price: From £750 per year for the entry paid tier; a free version is also available
enrichment.seo_title: SharpCloud Review 2026: Roadmapping and Visualization Pricing
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See how SharpCloud's Creator, Collaborator, and Read-Only licenses work for portfolio management and roadmapping, plus its pricing and 2026 Lumivero acquisition.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SharpCloud is a data visualization and strategic portfolio management platform that helps organizations turn spreadsheets, project plans, and other business data into interactive, story-based visualizations for roadmapping, portfolio management, risk management, and scenario planning. The company was founded in 2012 and is headquartered in London, United Kingdom. In January 2026, SharpCloud was acquired by Lumivero, a Denver, Colorado-based analytics software company also known for products such as XLSTAT and NVivo, and SharpCloud is now sold and supported as part of the Lumivero product portfolio. The platform serves more than 100 organizations and over 18,000 users globally, spanning use cases such as product roadmapping, enterprise portfolio management, innovation management, and Gantt or Kanban-style project tracking, alongside dashboards, custom reporting, and collaboration features like comments and mentions. SharpCloud connects to existing data sources through configurable connectors rather than requiring manual data re-entry, and its visual storytelling approach is designed to make complex interdependencies between projects, risks, and strategic goals easier for non-technical stakeholders to understand. Licensing is role-based across three tiers: Creator, for people who build and manage stories and connectors, with a 2-license minimum; Collaborator, for people who edit and maintain data, with a 5-license minimum; and Read-Only, for stakeholders who only need to view and comment, with a 50-license minimum. All licenses are billed annually, and public pricing for the entry paid tier starts at 750 British pounds per year. A free version is also available for evaluation.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is SharpCloud
paragraphs: SharpCloud is a data visualization and strategic portfolio management platform that converts spreadsheets, project plans, and other business data into interactive, story-based visualizations, aimed at making complex interdependencies between projects, risks, and strategic goals easier for stakeholders to understand at a glance.

Founded in 2012 and headquartered in London, United Kingdom, SharpCloud was acquired by Denver-based analytics software company Lumivero in January 2026, and is now sold and supported as part of Lumivero's broader analytics product portfolio, which also includes tools such as XLSTAT and NVivo.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: SharpCloud supports product roadmapping, enterprise portfolio management, innovation management, and risk management, alongside Gantt and Kanban-style views, custom dashboards, and reporting. Configurable connectors pull in data from existing sources rather than requiring manual re-entry.

Collaboration features such as commenting and mentions let stakeholders discuss specific parts of a visualization directly, and role-based licensing lets organizations control cost by separating story creators from data collaborators and read-only viewers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SharpCloud offers a free version for evaluation, alongside three paid, role-based annual license tiers: Creator, with a 2-license minimum, for people who build stories and configure connectors; Collaborator, with a 5-license minimum, for people who edit and maintain data; and Read-Only, with a 50-license minimum, for stakeholders who only need to view and comment.
Public pricing for the entry-level Creator tier starts at 750 British pounds per year; Collaborator and Read-Only pricing is not publicly listed and requires contacting sales.

features.title: Roadmapping and Portfolio Management
features.description: Supports product roadmaps and enterprise portfolio management in one visual view.

features.description: Pulls data from existing sources rather than requiring manual re-entry.

features.description: Tracks risks and innovation initiatives alongside broader strategic plans.

features.description: Offers timeline and Kanban-style views for project and initiative tracking.

features.title: Custom Dashboards and Reporting
features.description: Builds custom dashboards and reports from underlying project and portfolio data.

features.title: Scenario Planning
features.description: Lets teams model and compare different strategic scenarios visually.

features.title: Commenting and Collaboration
features.description: Stakeholders can comment and mention colleagues directly on visualizations.

pros: Makes complex project and portfolio interdependencies visually digestible for non-technical stakeholders
Configurable connectors reduce manual data re-entry from existing systems
Role-based licensing lets organizations control cost by usage type
Backed by established analytics vendor Lumivero following the January 2026 acquisition
cons: Public pricing is limited to the entry Creator tier; other tiers require a custom quote
Role-based minimums, such as the 50-license Read-Only minimum, may not suit very small teams
Less widely known than mainstream roadmapping tools such as Aha! or ProductPlan
Recent ownership change to Lumivero may bring future shifts in product direction or support

faqs.question: What is SharpCloud used for?
faqs.answer: SharpCloud is used for data visualization, strategic roadmapping, portfolio management, and scenario planning, turning spreadsheets and project data into interactive visual stories.

faqs.question: Who owns SharpCloud now?
faqs.answer: SharpCloud was acquired by Lumivero, a Denver-based analytics software company, in January 2026, and is now part of the Lumivero product portfolio.

faqs.question: How much does SharpCloud cost?
faqs.answer: SharpCloud's entry-level Creator license starts at 750 British pounds per year; Collaborator and Read-Only tier pricing requires contacting sales.

faqs.question: Does SharpCloud have a free version?
faqs.answer: Yes, SharpCloud offers a free version for evaluation alongside its paid tiers.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Creator, Collaborator, and Read-Only licenses?
faqs.answer: Creator licenses build and manage stories and connectors, Collaborator licenses edit and maintain data, and Read-Only licenses can only view and comment on stories.

faqs.question: Can SharpCloud connect to existing data sources?
faqs.answer: Yes, SharpCloud uses configurable connectors to pull in data from existing sources rather than requiring manual data entry.

faqs.question: Is SharpCloud good for portfolio management?
faqs.answer: Yes, SharpCloud is commonly used for enterprise portfolio management, roadmapping, and risk management use cases.

faqs.question: Where is SharpCloud based?
faqs.answer: SharpCloud was founded in 2012 and is headquartered in London, United Kingdom.
